description: This is an 800°C isothermal oxide map of the Fe-Mn-Al ternary alloy system, superimposing oxidation scale morphology regions on the alloy phase equilibrium section. The triangular plot uses weight percent as units, with the bottom axis representing manganese content (0 to 40 wt.%) and left axis representing aluminum content (0 to 25 wt.%). Four composition regions with distinct scale morphologies are marked: Region I forms alumina with bulky oxide nodules, Region II forms alumina with bulky Mn-rich oxides, Region III forms continuous protective alumina scales (corresponding to the most oxidation-resistant fully ferritic alloys with Fe-(5–10) wt.% Mn-(6–10) wt.% Al composition), Region IV forms alumina with small Mn-rich oxide nodules. Alloy phase regions including α (ferrite), α+γ (ferrite + austenite), γ (austenite), α+β-Mn, γ+β-Mn are also labeled. The map demonstrates that austenite phase impairs oxidation resistance, and correlates alloy composition, matrix phase, and oxidation performance to guide composition design for high-temperature oxidation-resistant Fe-Mn-Al alloys.
